selected_title: Alternative Decentralization Models and the Chilean Experience
question: What mechanism does Mario Marcel identify in “Alternative Decentralization Models and the Chilean Experience” that deepens the evidence from “Administrative reforms in Peru, 2003-2006 : decentralization in name only?”?

evidence_spans.text: Since the beginning of the 1990s, decentralization processes in Latin America have experienced both advances and setbacks. The enthusiasm over decentralization that accompanied the democratic process was in many cases followed by crisis and restrictions to the faculties of subnational governments.

learned_claims: The inspected source addresses Decentralization, Enthusiasm through the evidence summarized in the preserved span.
The abstract supplies a direct evidence span of 41 words; interpretation remains bounded to that span.
